notes.structure: Contracts come in three kinds: Arcadian (grouped into six Regalia), Court (the four seasonal courts), and Goblin. Each Contract has a tier — common or royal (goblin contracts are their own tier).
notes.regalia: Every seeming has one favored Regalia, and a changeling chooses a second favored Regalia at creation. Common Arcadian Contracts from a favored Regalia cost less; Royal Arcadian Contracts may only be learned from a favored Regalia.
notes.chargen: A changeling begins with four Common Contracts (Common Regalia, Common Court, or Goblin), of which at least two must come from a favored Regalia, plus two Royal Contracts from her court or a favored Regalia. No more than two Goblin Contracts at creation.
notes.seemingClauses: Each Arcadian Contract grants extra effects to two specific seemings; a changeling of that seeming gains the effect free. Court Contracts key off Mantle and have no seeming clauses.
notes.accuracy: Contract text is summarized for in-game listing. Consult Changeling: The Lost 2e for full mechanical text, dice pools, and roll results before adjudicating an effect.

regalia.name: Crown
regalia.description: The Regalia of leadership, command, and rulership. Fairest favor the Crown.

regalia.name: Jewels
regalia.description: The Regalia of crafting, objects, fortune, and fine work. Wizened favor Jewels.

regalia.name: Mirror
regalia.description: The Regalia of perception, disguise, reflection, and secrets. Darklings favor the Mirror.

regalia.name: Shield
regalia.description: The Regalia of protection, endurance, and defense. Ogres favor the Shield.

regalia.name: Steed
regalia.description: The Regalia of travel, movement, pursuit, and the Hedge. Beasts favor the Steed.

regalia.name: Sword
regalia.description: The Regalia of combat, wrath, and the elements. Elementals favor the Sword.

contracts.name: Hostile Takeover
contracts.effect: The changeling declares her right to be on any premises, bypassing mundane security, opening doors, and keeping animal and hobgoblin sentries out of her way, extending the benefit to companions equal to her Presence in line of sight.
contracts.loophole: The changeling carries an object that belongs to the dwelling's owner or main resident.

seemingClauses.effect: The Beast persuades animal sentries to actively serve her.
The Fairest gains two bonus dice on a Clash of Wills with a supernatural dwelling's owner.

contracts.name: Mask of Superiority
contracts.effect: People who see the changeling believe he is a respected member of an organization they belong to, as though he had dots in its Status Merit equal to his Presence.
contracts.loophole: The changeling loosely dresses to copy the organization's dress code, which need not be identical.

seemingClauses.effect: Reduce all starting Doors by one for Social maneuvering the changeling initiates on affected characters.
The Bruiser appears as security, adding the successes rolled as bonus dice to Intimidation against anyone who believes the ruse, max +5.

contracts.name: Paralyzing Presence
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Intimidation + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling grows larger and more terrible in the target's mind and overwhelms her, inflicting the Insensate Tilt.
contracts.loophole: The victim is otherwise alone, and the changeling touches him.

seemingClauses.effect: The Mountebank may use Manipulation instead of Presence to invoke this Contract.
The Unicorn gains a three-die bonus to invoke this Contract.

contracts.name: Summon the Loyal Servant
contracts.effect: The changeling lets a drop of his blood fall onto a substance to animate a small loyal servitor as intelligent as a dog that carries out simple commands and notices threats until the scene ends or it is destroyed.

seemingClauses.effect: The Sprite may merge with the servitor, substituting its Attributes for his own where he chooses and becoming immune to its element.
The Sovereign may bind one servitor at a time to a shared Hollow, keeping it animate as long as it stays within.

contracts.name: Tumult
contracts.effect: Inscribing his target's true name on a leaf folded into an origami bird, the changeling inflicts one temporary mental Condition (Disoriented, Guilty, Lost, Paranoid, Obsession, Spooked, Stoic, or Withdrawn) per success.
contracts.loophole: The changeling planted an origami bird on the target's person this scene.

seemingClauses.effect: The Fairest adds Inspired, Swooned, and Wanton to the list of Conditions.
The Ogre adds Bestial, Cowed, and Frightened to the list of Conditions.

contracts.name: Discreet Summons
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Persuasion + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling pulls a small familiar item from a container, or opens a door to summon a low-Wyrd hobgoblin that performs one task, both vanishing at scene's end.
contracts.loophole: The changeling pulls an object from a location belonging to or guarded by an enemy, keeping a difficult promise made to the hobgoblin.

seemingClauses.effect: A Wisp may pull the object or summon the hobgoblin from any sufficiently large dark space or shadow.
The summoned hobgoblin serves the changeling until the sun next crosses the horizon.

contracts.name: Mastermind's Gambit
contracts.effect: Speaking his goal aloud, the changeling creates a plan or repository that counts as equipment granting a +5 bonus, lasting until the chapter ends or the plan definitively resolves.
contracts.loophole: The changeling outlines the hierarchy, index, or plan on paper at least 50 years old.

seemingClauses.effect: The Unbound can make a repository last the story, expanding it to encompass one Mental Skill Specialty shaped to his elemental affinity.
The Sovereign can also create an organization geared to counter his enemy's influence.

contracts.name: Pipes of the Beastcaller
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Animal Ken + Wyrd vs. Resolve + Composure
contracts.effect: The changeling plays a silver flute to summon all animals of one species within his Animal Ken in miles, giving them simple commands they obey.
contracts.loophole: While playing, the changeling dances to mimic the movements of the animals he wants to summon.

seemingClauses.effect: The Grim gains an empathic link to the animals, giving instructions from a distance and sensing their condition and location.
The Fairest can pay an extra Glamour to summon and command a second species.

contracts.name: The Royal Court
contracts.effect: Setting himself up as leader of a meeting, the changeling prevents anyone present from inflicting bodily harm, contesting supernatural coercion toward violence (but not violence already begun).
contracts.loophole: The changeling stands and makes a speech lasting at least five minutes.

seemingClauses.effect: Protection extends to Social interactions, preventing intimidation or threats while still allowing goodwill.
The Hatter's protection extends to mental harm, including Clarity attacks but not breaking points.

contracts.name: Blessing of Perfection
contracts.effect: The changeling caresses an object to replace its equipment bonus with her Wyrd, or encourages a person to replace his Crafts, Medicine, or Computer rating with her Wyrd.
contracts.loophole: The changeling accepts payment to enact this Contract, then buries the coins or traded object.

seemingClauses.effect: The Fairest may apply the effect to another's Expression, Persuasion, or Socialize rolls.
A blessed object keeps the bonus to all rolls to use it for the scene.

contracts.name: Changing Fortunes
contracts.effect: The changeling whispers a tale to the wind to add or subtract two dice from a target's next roll, or shift his exceptional-success threshold, per success — once per chapter per target.

seemingClauses.effect: The target suffers the Shaken Condition whenever he uses the cursed pool, until the sun next crosses the horizon.
After seeing the impacted roll, the player may force a reroll, and the second roll stands.

contracts.name: Light-Shy
contracts.effect: The changeling becomes invisible to the mind across all senses (though recording tech still detects her), ending if she takes aggressive action or harms anyone.
contracts.loophole: The changeling stands unmoving in darkness or deep shadow for one minute.

seemingClauses.effect: The Bewitched erases herself so completely that even recording technology no longer detects her.
The Domovoi can hide an object so people make up rational excuses for why it's gone, until it is moved.

contracts.name: Murkblur
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Subterfuge + Wyrd vs. Wits + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The target beholds the impossible beauty of Arcadia and suffers the Blinded Tilt in both eyes.
contracts.loophole: The changeling eats a creature's eye while invoking the Contract.

seemingClauses.effect: The Unbound may instead make the target feel enveloped by an element, suffering appropriate Tilts.
The Wizened cuts off the target's senses so completely he also suffers the Disoriented Condition.

contracts.name: Trivial Reworking
contracts.effect: The changeling cloaks a mundane item up to Size 3 in her Mask and changes its appearance — a purely psychological illusion that fails against detection or those who see through the Mask.
contracts.loophole: The character touched another object similar to the illusory one this scene.

seemingClauses.effect: Attempts to detect the forgery subtract dice equal to the Mountebank's Manipulation.
The Wizened can affect an object of any Size.

contracts.name: Spinning Wheel
contracts.duration: Until the lunar month ends or the event passes
contracts.effect: The changeling guides a target's future toward a named, probable experience, granting bonus dice to rolls leading there and a penalty to rolls obstructing it, max ±5.

seemingClauses.effect: The Muse gains three bonus dice to invoke this Contract when encouraging a positive experience.

contracts.name: Changeling Hours
contracts.effect: The changeling draws a clock in the air to rewind an object's damage, speed its decay to inflict Durability-ignoring damage, or freeze it immobile and immune to change.
contracts.loophole: The changeling names a former (not current) owner of the object.

seemingClauses.effect: May also affect instances of her element, such as an ever-burning flame or a restored flower.
May make the freeze permanent for an extra Willpower; overriding powers prompt a Clash of Wills.

contracts.name: Dance of the Toys
contracts.effect: The changeling brings a mechanical device within (10 x Wyrd) yards to life to follow one simple command, using invocation successes as its action pool.
contracts.loophole: The changeling inscribes a name on the object and calls it to life (a new name each time).

seemingClauses.effect: The Beast imbues the device with animal-like intelligence for more complex commands.
The Shrewd can compel the device beyond its normal range at Speed equal to her Wyrd.

contracts.name: Hidden Reality
contracts.effect: The changeling alters one minor feature of a lifeless object so it could always have been that way (a hidden latch, a door on a wall), vanishing at scene's end or when unobserved.
contracts.loophole: The changeling makes a show of searching, then 'finds' the secret feature with surprise (a full minute to invoke).

seemingClauses.effect: The Sovereign instead changes one nearby person's Virtue or Vice, contested by Resolve + Wyrd.
A Hatter may pay an extra Glamour to create whole new objects reasonable to the area.

contracts.name: Stealing the Solid Reflection
contracts.effect: The changeling steals a reflection and turns it into a solid object, angering the Hedge-dwelling mirror people whose things she takes.
contracts.loophole: The changeling coaxed the owner of the original to express a sense of debt to her this scene.

seemingClauses.effect: The stolen reflection is friendly and deferential to the Fairest.
For an extra Glamour the reflection retains any mystical properties of the original.

contracts.name: Tatterdemalion's Workshop
contracts.effect: The changeling weaves an object into being from the unlikeliest of forgotten scraps and materials, as the Gentry do.

contracts.name: Glimpse of a Distant Mirror
contracts.effect: The changeling turns a reflective surface into a window looking out through another surface that has previously reflected his face.
contracts.loophole: The changeling looks into a mirror belonging to someone who has sworn enmity against him.

seemingClauses.effect: The Savage may see out of any surface reflecting someone who has pledged to him or invoked his name.
The Darkling can also hear through the window, and may let those on the other side see and hear him.

contracts.name: Know the Competition
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Socialize + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling challenges an opponent to a game and, on winning, learns her Virtue, Vice, and one Aspiration.
contracts.loophole: The changeling goaded his target into challenging him to a game.

seemingClauses.effect: Once before dawn the Beast can think of the target to know exactly where she is and what she is doing.
The Darkling can observe multiple opponents at once, up to his Wyrd, in a many-player game.

contracts.name: Portents and Visions
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Occult + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling sees a vision of the most important past or future event, relevant to his interests, that concerns the target.
contracts.loophole: The changeling tears up a picture of the target.

seemingClauses.effect: The Bewitched may instead see a past transgression and inflict the Guilty Condition about it.
The Torrent may see the next violent event and grant the target the Giant Merit for a scene if it comes to pass.

contracts.name: Read Lucidity
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Empathy + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling reads another's mental stability at a glance — maximum and current Clarity and which Clarity Conditions she suffers.
contracts.loophole: The changeling touches the target, skin-to-skin.

seemingClauses.effect: On an exceptional success the Beast may roll his successes as a Clarity attack against the target.
On an exceptional success the Darkling lends the target Defense equal to her Wits against the next Clarity attack.

contracts.name: Walls Have Ears
contracts.effect: The changeling tells an object a secret from Arcadia, and it tells him a secret in return about its construction, use, or who last handled it.
contracts.loophole: The changeling is in public, among mortals who can hear him speak his secret aloud.

seemingClauses.effect: The Wisp also gains the Informed Condition about the object's owner.
The Wizened may pay extra Glamour to view further back in time, naming who was present.

contracts.name: Props and Scenery
contracts.effect: The changeling transforms into a desired inanimate object up to his own Size, gaining extra benefits per success such as Durability, mobility, or Size changes.
contracts.loophole: The changeling is in plain sight of multiple others, but no one is looking at him at the moment he invokes it.

seemingClauses.effect: The Darkling may extend the duration until the sun next crosses the horizon.
The Gargoyle may assume the form of objects up to double his Size.

contracts.name: Reflections of the Past
contracts.effect: The changeling watches a past event replay in a reflective surface, up to a scene's worth of events from a specified time.
contracts.loophole: The changeling lets his blood (after taking 1L this scene) fall on the surface, which absorbs it before showing the vision.

seemingClauses.effect: The Mountebank may use his kenning to detect supernatural phenomena in the reflected events.
The Fairest may inflict the Leveraged Condition on a character in the vision.

contracts.name: Riddle-Kith
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Larceny + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling alters his own or another Lost's mien to emulate a different kith without gaining its blessing.
contracts.loophole: The changeling gifted something to a changeling of that kith this scene, getting only gratitude in return.

seemingClauses.effect: The Darkling's target truly becomes the kith impersonated, swapping its blessing for the false one's.
The Elemental may also make the target appear to be a different seeming.

contracts.name: Skinmask
contracts.effect: The changeling recites three things he knows about a target and assumes her appearance, copying both Mask and mien if she is a changeling.
contracts.loophole: The changeling holds an object belonging to the character he wants to mimic.

seemingClauses.effect: The Bewitched may buy a mastered visage as Alternate Identity and shift to it reflexively after emulating that person three times.
The Fairest knows whether an action fits the copied target and gains three bonus dice to mimic her behavior.

contracts.name: Unravel the Tapestry
contracts.effect: The changeling rewinds the last ten seconds (or one turn of Initiative), changing his own actions while others retake their originals.
contracts.loophole: The changeling incurred a debt this scene which he has not yet repaid.

seemingClauses.effect: The Wisp may reflexively move up to his Stealth in yards before time begins anew.
The Wizened gains one surprise attack against any viable target.

contracts.name: Cloak of Night
contracts.effect: The changeling commands night and shadow to shield herself and her companions.

contracts.name: Shared Burden
contracts.effect: The changeling sheds her own blood to heal a target, healing two points of his damage for each point of lethal she inflicts on herself.
contracts.loophole: The changeling holds a strip of skin, four inches or longer, of the creature that inflicted the injury.

seemingClauses.effect: The Gargoyle heals three points of damage per point of lethal inflicted.
The Hatter heals lethal damage first, then bashing.

contracts.name: Thorns and Brambles
contracts.effect: Brambles grow around the changeling in a radius equal to her Wyrd and follow her, producing one chosen defensive effect.
contracts.loophole: The changeling litters a handful of Hedge thorns behind her.

seemingClauses.effect: The Wisp gains the Glamour drained by Leechweed.
Characters in the Briarpatch take an Athletics penalty equal to the Ogre's Strength.

contracts.name: Trapdoor Spider's Trick
contracts.duration: One scene or until the sun crosses the horizon
contracts.effect: The changeling cloaks an opening with Glamour to make it seem impassable or absent.
contracts.loophole: The changeling lures an enemy or rival through the portal first.

seemingClauses.effect: The Ogre marks the illusion with a symbol that lets allies see through it automatically.
The Domovoi's illusion encompasses all senses; only supernatural perception pierces it.

contracts.name: Hedgewall
contracts.effect: The changeling summons a massive castle of thorns and brambles for protection.
contracts.loophole: The changeling plants a seed or sapling from the Hedge immediately before using it.

seemingClauses.effect: The Beast's Hedgewall is intoxicating; enemies suffer -2 on Resolve rolls inside.
Hedgewall lasts until the sun next passes the horizon.

contracts.name: Fortifying Presence
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Empathy + Wyrd vs. Resolve + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling spends meaningful time with a target to heal two points of mild Clarity damage or one of severe.
contracts.loophole: The changeling professes friendship to the target before multiple witnesses while invoking it.

seemingClauses.effect: The target gains Defense equal to the Muse's Presence against the next Clarity attack this story.
The Terrible may reverse the effect, making a Clarity attack with a pool equal to her Empathy.

contracts.name: Pure Clarity
contracts.effect: The changeling takes one action this scene that would normally prompt a breaking point without suffering one.
contracts.loophole: The changeling dons a metal gauntlet on one hand and a silk glove on the other.

seemingClauses.effect: The Fairest may also shield herself from situations inflicted upon her.
The Ogre may protect one of her companions instead.

contracts.name: Vow of No Compromise
contracts.effect: With a touch and a spoken promise, the changeling downgrades one level of a target's aggravated damage to lethal and gains the Stoic Condition.
contracts.loophole: The changeling destroys a representation of the True Fae, or kills one of their servants.

seemingClauses.effect: The Gargoyle gains the Inspired Condition about punishing whoever caused the damage.
The Sprite needn't touch a target surrounded by or submerged in her element.

contracts.name: Whispers of Morning
contracts.effect: The changeling becomes intangible and weightless, untouchable save by magic, and passes through physical barriers at will.
contracts.loophole: The changeling carries no weapons and wears no armor.

seemingClauses.effect: The Bruiser can carry one person on her back and extend the effect to him.
The Shrewd can pick up Size 1 objects, which become intangible too.

contracts.name: Boon of the Scuttling Spider
contracts.effect: The world flattens so the changeling moves at normal Speed along walls, ceilings, or slick surfaces strong enough to bear his weight.
contracts.loophole: The changeling swallows a live spider.

seemingClauses.effect: The Courser gains the spider's webbing and may restrain in a grapple as if he'd rolled an exceptional success.
The Darkling sticks to shadows, gaining two bonus dice on Stealth while scuttling.

contracts.name: Nevertread
contracts.effect: The changeling cloaks his footprints so all his tracks change, making him untrackable except by supernatural means (a Clash of Wills).
contracts.loophole: The changeling leaves a deliberate clue of his passing that reveals his identity if found.

seemingClauses.effect: Companions traveling with him benefit too, up to double his Stealth.
The Domovoi's footsteps leave traps, per Safe Place with dots equal to his Dexterity.

contracts.name: Dreamsteps
contracts.dicePool: Intelligence + Empathy + Wyrd vs. Bastion's Fortification
contracts.effect: The changeling touches a sleeping target and steps into her dreams through the Gate of Ivory.

contracts.name: Pathfinder
contracts.effect: The changeling draws a compass on his hand and knows the distance and direction of the nearest chosen Hedge feature (Goblin Market, Hollow, goblin fruit, Gate of Horn).
contracts.loophole: The changeling took 1L this scene from a part of the Hedge, such as a thorn or hobgoblin's knife.

seemingClauses.effect: The Grim also senses nearby creatures: how many and whether they mean ill.
The Wizened knows whether nearby goblin fruits are beneficial and gleans their types.

contracts.name: Seven-League Leap
contracts.effect: The changeling clears a jumping trajectory of 10 yards per dot of Wyrd on a Strength + Athletics roll.
contracts.loophole: The changeling wears boots he stole from an enemy this scene.

seemingClauses.effect: Increases the Beast's Speed by 10 for the scene; in a foot chase he gains the Edge next turn.
The Ogre can leap as an unarmed attack, +2 Strength and the Knocked Down Tilt on a hit.

contracts.name: Chrysalis
contracts.effect: The changeling transforms into one of two chosen animals (Size 1-7), gaining its form, senses, and movement, and can speak with that species.
contracts.loophole: The changeling is in the animal's natural habitat, near enough to touch one.

seemingClauses.effect: The character chooses two additional animal forms on purchase.
The character may choose animals up to Size 15.

contracts.name: Leaping Toward Nightfall
contracts.dicePool: Intelligence + Occult + Wyrd vs. Resolve + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling sends an object up to Size 10, or a character, forward in time up to days equal to successes, vanishing and reappearing in place.
contracts.loophole: The changeling destroys a piece of the target (hair, nail, fluid), an Icon, or something from the target's dreams.

seemingClauses.effect: Time's ravages inflict the Disoriented Condition on sentient targets.
On arrival the target doesn't remember the scene of invocation, permanently unless reversed.

contracts.name: Flickering Hours
contracts.duration: Until the changeling exits the Hedge
contracts.effect: The changeling slows time by half or speeds it twice as fast for his traveling companions, granting Fleet of Foot up to his Wyrd (max 3).
contracts.loophole: The changeling smashes an antique clock or other old timepiece as he invokes it.

seemingClauses.effect: The Savage may freely extend the Contract to anyone he meets on the journey.
The path becomes nearly impassable in his wake, suffering the Ice Tilt fitted to his element.

contracts.name: Mirror Walk
contracts.effect: The changeling leaves a bloody print on a mirror and steps through to any previously touched mirror in the mortal world or Hedge, with companions in a linked chain.

seemingClauses.effect: The Beast may end the Contract in mirror space, gaining two bonus dice to navigate it for the scene.
The Sprite becomes mirror-like after exiting, invisible for minutes equal to successes.

contracts.name: Elemental Weapon
contracts.effect: The changeling shapes a nearby element into any archaic weapon, adding bonuses (weapon modifier, reduced Initiative penalty, increased range) per success.

seemingClauses.effect: The Mountebank's shadow weapon can inflict the Blinded Tilt instead of damage on a hit.
A weapon of her affinity makes her terrifying; she may roll to inflict the Stunned Tilt.

contracts.name: Might of the Terrible Brute
contracts.effect: On winning a contested grapple, the changeling reduces the opponent's Strength by one and adds it to her own, up to +5.
contracts.loophole: The character is grappling multiple opponents at once.

seemingClauses.effect: The Beast may steal Dexterity instead, choosing which on invocation.
The Elemental also gains the effects of the Giant Merit.

contracts.name: Overpowering Dread
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Intimidation + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling curses a target with the Frightened Condition by methodically destroying an object while staring at him.
contracts.loophole: The changeling stands in shadows and takes the target by surprise.

seemingClauses.effect: The Elemental may dread one extra target by infusing the air itself.
The Fairest may frighten then comfort the target, gaining the Swooned Condition regarding her.

contracts.name: Primal Glory
contracts.effect: Touching an element, the changeling gains immunity to its mundane damage, half from magic, and 1/1 armor that deals 1L per turn to melee attackers.
contracts.loophole: The changeling consumes the element (drinking water, eating dirt, inhaling smoke).

seemingClauses.effect: Affinity-element armor deals its damage to anyone within her Wyrd in yards instead.
The Ogre is fully immune to magical instances of the chosen element.

contracts.name: Touch of Wrath
contracts.effect: The changeling touches an object and deals one point of damage to it per success, splintering stone or wrecking metal.
contracts.loophole: The object's owner stole from or cheated the changeling this scene.

seemingClauses.effect: The Torrent's touch deals double damage to objects of entirely natural materials.
The Wizened need only look at the item to use this Contract.

contracts.name: Elemental Fury
contracts.effect: The changeling inflicts one Environmental Tilt of her choice per Glamour spent within 20 yards, while remaining immune.
contracts.loophole: The changeling publicly rants, swears vengeance, or proclaims her rage to all present.

seemingClauses.effect: Also deals bashing equal to her Presence to anyone caught in the area on invocation.
The Muse may protect targets she can see from the Tilt.

contracts.name: Oathbreaker's Punishment
contracts.effect: The changeling senses the target's worst unatoned broken promise and sculpts one waking nightmare per success around it.
contracts.loophole: The target swore a promise to the changeling this scene that is still in effect.

seemingClauses.effect: The Shrewd gleans all details of the broken oath.
The Elemental can publicize the broken promise, inflicting the Notoriety Condition.

contracts.name: Red Revenge
contracts.effect: The changeling gains +3 Initiative, Intimidation, and Physical Attributes, 3/3 armor, and the Berserk Condition.
contracts.loophole: The changeling exacts vengeance for a loved one or friend.

seemingClauses.effect: The Torrent gains +1 weapon modifier on Brawl and Weaponry attacks.
The Ogre's attacks also inflict the Knocked Down Tilt.

contracts.name: Relentless Pursuit
contracts.duration: Until the sun next passes the horizon
contracts.effect: The changeling knows the direction, rough distance, and realm of a named target, triggering a Clash of Wills if he evades by supernatural means.
contracts.loophole: The changeling holds a piece of the target (hair, nail, fluid), an Icon, or something from his dreams.

seemingClauses.effect: The Savage also gets a brief vision of what the target is doing on invocation.
The Elemental may hide from the sun to prolong the Contract and gain a chase modifier up to +5.

contracts.name: Thief of Reason
contracts.effect: The changeling rolls invocation successes as a Clarity attack, also costing the target a Willpower point if Clarity damage results.
contracts.loophole: The changeling coaxed the subject into doubting her own sanity aloud this scene.

seemingClauses.effect: Successes add to the changeling's Wyrd for the Clarity damage pool.
A Fairest may pay one Glamour to postpone the effect until a chosen trigger within a day.

contracts.name: Cupid's Arrow
contracts.dicePool: Wits + Empathy + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling learns the target's most ardent desire and connected Conditions, and may replace it with one of his choice for the scene.
contracts.loophole: The changeling is holding a living ivy flower.

contracts.name: Dreams of the Earth
contracts.effect: The changeling sings to seeds and buds, accelerating dormant plant life toward awakening.

contracts.name: Gift of Warm Breath
contracts.effect: The changeling touches a willing target, shedding all fatigue penalties, fatigue Tilts, temporary illness, and bashing wounds.
contracts.loophole: The changeling gives the target something to eat or drink that he made himself.

contracts.name: Spring's Kiss
contracts.effect: Rain falls as the changeling wishes within (Mantle) miles, inflicting the Heavy Rain Tilt (or Flooded for an extra Glamour).
contracts.loophole: The changeling is wearing bright yellow rain boots and cap.

contracts.name: Wyrd-Faced Stranger
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Subterfuge + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling takes the appearance of whomever his target most wants to see, gaining (Mantle) bonus dice when acting out of character for that person.
contracts.loophole: The changeling has something of emotional value to his target in his pocket.

contracts.name: Blessing of Spring
contracts.dicePool: Intelligence + Medicine + Mantle vs. Stamina + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The target gains a spring's growth, healing all wounds and curing illness/poison that time would yield; changelings also shed a temporary Clarity Condition for the scene.
contracts.loophole: The changeling decorates his target with ribbons and dances around her.

contracts.name: Gift of Warm Blood
contracts.effect: Each success downgrades one wound (aggravated to lethal, lethal to bashing, bashing to healed).
contracts.loophole: The changeling adds his own blood, taking one lethal damage.

contracts.name: Pandora's Gift
contracts.duration: Until the sun next crosses the horizon
contracts.effect: The changeling shapes a target's desires into a crafted object (Build Equipment in half time), granting three bonus dice when used as a bribe or bargaining chip.
contracts.loophole: The target gave the changeling a valuable or sentimental gift, no strings attached, this scene.

contracts.name: Prince of Ivy
contracts.effect: Vines erupt to ensnare enemies, letting the changeling make one grapple per turn against any target within three yards of a plant.
contracts.loophole: The changeling stands barefoot in soil and sprinkles his blood on the earth (1 lethal).

contracts.name: Waking the Inner Fae
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Expression + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The target accepts a wreath and gains the Wanton Condition, letting the changeling regain Willpower once per scene when he tempts that target into action.
contracts.loophole: The changeling told the target one of his own secret desires last scene.

contracts.name: Baleful Sense
contracts.dicePool: Wits + Intimidation + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling learns the target's greatest source of wrath and connected Conditions, and may redirect that anger toward a chosen subject for the scene.
contracts.loophole: The changeling coaxed her target into screaming at her this scene.

contracts.name: Child of the Hearth
contracts.effect: The sun's warmth fills or withdraws from a room-sized area, inflicting Extreme Heat or Cold while the changeling stays immune.
contracts.loophole: The character blows on a fading ember or spark.

contracts.name: Helios' Light
contracts.effect: The changeling emits true sunlight over (Mantle x 20) yards, inflicting Blinded on anyone looking at her and half damage to sunlight-vulnerable creatures.
contracts.loophole: The changeling is in pitch darkness and cannot see.

contracts.name: High Summer's Zeal
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Persuasion + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: A foe trying to flee must spend Willpower or keep opposing the changeling, taking only actions toward winning and immune to Beaten Down.
contracts.loophole: The changeling's opponent drew first blood, goaded or not.

contracts.name: Vigilance of Ares
contracts.effect: The changeling automatically detects ambushes, hidden traps, and surprise attacks, and gains Initiative equal to her Mantle.
contracts.loophole: The changeling executed a martial exercise this scene.

contracts.name: Fiery Tongue
contracts.effect: A verbal rebuke inflicts successes as bashing (lethal vs fae), removes two Doors, and worsens the target's impression to hostile.
contracts.loophole: The changeling asserts dominance by basing a claim of superiority on something that must be true.

contracts.name: Flames of Summer
contracts.effect: The changeling adds two dice to Physical rolls, ignores wound penalties, and need not roll to stay conscious at her last Health box.
contracts.loophole: The changeling violently crushes a chunk of ice with her teeth before invoking it.

contracts.name: Helios' Judgment
contracts.effect: The changeling hurls a ray of sunlight as a thrown weapon dealing (Mantle)L (10/30/50), returning to her hand each turn.
contracts.loophole: The target is wearing or touching pure gold.

contracts.name: Solstice Revelation
contracts.effect: Light floods a 30-yard radius, stripping mundane concealment, contesting supernatural hiding, and weakening Masks so anyone may see through with Wits + Composure.
contracts.loophole: The changeling loudly reveals a secret she was intentionally keeping, for all present to hear.

contracts.name: Sunburnt Heart
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Persuasion + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling inflicts the Berserk Condition and gains (Mantle) bonus dice to direct the target's fury onto someone other than herself.
contracts.loophole: The changeling shines a bright light into the target's eyes (avoided by a reflexive Wits + Composure).

contracts.name: Tale of the Baba Yaga
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Subterfuge + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling inflicts the Shaken Condition on some or all of his audience; on an exceptional success they must spend Willpower to act against him.
contracts.loophole: The changeling weaves the Contract into an urban legend all listeners already know.

contracts.name: Witches' Intuition
contracts.dicePool: Wits + Subterfuge + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling learns the subject's greatest fear and connected Conditions, and may replace it with one of his choice for the scene.
contracts.loophole: The changeling eats part of the target — a droplet of blood, a torn nail, a stolen hair.

contracts.name: Twilight's Harbinger
contracts.duration: Until the lunar month ends or the ending comes to pass
contracts.effect: A creature of omens visits to warn the changeling shortly before a chosen circumstance or event comes to an end.
contracts.loophole: The changeling carries something meaningfully connected to someone vital to the chosen event.

contracts.name: Famine's Bulwark
contracts.effect: Per success the changeling asks the Storyteller one yes/no question about the situation, getting true answers to all but one.
contracts.loophole: The changeling eats the most recent fruit of a plant he raised himself.

contracts.name: Mien of the Baba Yaga
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Intimidation + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: On entering the target's sight she appears as his greatest fear; he gains Frightened and must spend Willpower to take any dice action.
contracts.loophole: The changeling whispers the specific name or title of the target's greatest fear.

contracts.name: Riding the Falling Leaves
contracts.effect: The changeling becomes a resilient spray of autumn leaves that flies at Speed -3, slips through small openings, and Dodges reflexively once per turn.
contracts.loophole: The changeling catches a naturally falling leaf, paper, or feather at the moment of invocation.

contracts.name: Sorcerer's Rebuke
contracts.effect: The touched target loses points of Glamour or another supernatural power source equal to the successes rolled.
contracts.loophole: The changeling made a grand speech or threat at least 30 seconds long this scene, warning of his might.

contracts.name: Tasting the Harvest
contracts.effect: Targets become immune to natural fear and gain (Mantle) bonus dice to contest supernatural fear, plus a die against things that scare them.
contracts.loophole: The changeling has legitimately jump-scared one of the targets this scene.

contracts.name: The Dragon Knows
contracts.dicePool: Wits + Empathy + Mantle vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling tastes the target's sorrow to learn his deepest regret and connected Conditions, and may redirect it toward a new subject for the scene.
contracts.loophole: The character looks into the subject's eyes for a few uninterrupted seconds.

contracts.name: Heart of Ice
contracts.effect: The changeling's heart freezes so that cold cannot harm her.

contracts.name: Ice Queen's Call
contracts.effect: The changeling spits a frozen seed that grows into a cold spot inflicting the Blizzard Tilt out to (Mantle x 20) yards, to which she is immune.
contracts.loophole: The character sees, or makes, someone shiver.

contracts.name: Slipknot Dreams
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Empathy + Mantle vs. Resolve + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling soothes her target's regrets without erasing memory, granting him the Swooned Condition toward her.
contracts.loophole: The target accepted a valuable or sentimental gift from the changeling this scene.

contracts.name: Touch of Winter
contracts.effect: The changeling flash-freezes the surface of a body of water in an expanding frozen area, supporting people equal to her Mantle.
contracts.loophole: The character melts a handful of ice completely to liquid before invoking it.

contracts.name: Ermine's Winter Coat
contracts.effect: The changeling gains +3 Stealth, -3 to attacks against her, halved Wyrd for fae tracking among non-fae, and +3 to Clashes of Wills to seem mortal.
contracts.loophole: The changeling digs a hole or makes a small space and hides in it while activating it.

contracts.name: Fallow Fields
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Empathy + Mantle vs. Resolve + Wyrd
contracts.duration: Days equal to the changeling's Mantle
contracts.effect: The target forgets what love feels like, cannot regain Willpower through Virtue or Vice, and gains the Broken Condition.
contracts.loophole: The name the changeling wrote down is one of the target's Touchstones.

contracts.name: Field of Regret
contracts.effect: Hedge ghosts pass through visible targets, inflicting successes as lethal and stripping one Willpower each.
contracts.loophole: The changeling sings a melancholic song and lets a ghost possess her for at least ten seconds.

contracts.name: Mantle of Frost
contracts.effect: The changeling radiates cold over 20 yards with escalating Extreme Cold penalties that build into lethal damage and the Immobilized Tilt, while staying immune.
contracts.loophole: The changeling dramatically drops her coat like a mantle, then destroys it.

contracts.name: Winter's Curse
contracts.dicePool: Presence + Survival + Mantle vs. Resolve + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The target's heart freezes: he can't join teamwork, spend or regain Willpower, or suffer breaking points, abandons allies and Aspirations, and is held to Average impressions.
contracts.loophole: The changeling swallows an ice cube whole right before invoking it.

contracts.name: Blessing of Forgetfulness
contracts.dicePool: Manipulation + Subterfuge + Wyrd vs. Composure + Wyrd
contracts.effect: The changeling erases a single continuous incident from a target's memory, replacing it with a harmless false one.
contracts.loophole: The event caused pain to the target, but the changeling rendered aid or showed him kindness.

contracts.name: Glib Tongue
contracts.effect: The changeling tells one lie a mortal believes (unless demonstrably false), or adds his Wyrd as bonus dice (max +5) to a Subterfuge roll against a supernatural target.
contracts.loophole: The changeling tells another target a lie that harms someone, in the same scene.

contracts.name: Goblin's Eye
contracts.effect: The changeling's kenning may trigger a Clash of Wills to see through magical concealment and ask one question per success about an uncovered supernatural subject.
contracts.loophole: The changeling burns an owl's feather and smudges the ashes on his eyelids.

contracts.name: Goblin's Luck
contracts.effect: When making a random guess among finite possibilities, the changeling enjoys a small instance of luck that makes the guess pay off.
contracts.loophole: The character freely extends this Contract to also benefit an unfriendly rival or enemy.

contracts.name: Huntsman's Clarion
contracts.effect: From within the Hedge, the changeling knows when a True Fae, Huntsman, other Arcadian, or her own fetch is within her Wyrd in miles (but not exactly where).
contracts.loophole: The changeling blindfolds herself for the duration.

contracts.name: Lost Visage
contracts.effect: At scene's end the changeling erases himself from mortal memory of it (and supernatural memory on a Clash of Wills); True Fae and Huntsmen are immune.
contracts.loophole: The changeling leaves one of his fairly unique possessions at the scene.

contracts.name: Mantle Mask
contracts.effect: The changeling cosmetically dims her Mantle and assumes another court's (up to her true Mantle), or appears to have no Mantle at all.
contracts.loophole: The changeling decorates her skin with symbols of the court whose Mantle she wants to mimic.

contracts.name: Sight of Truth and Lies
contracts.effect: The changeling recognizes any mundane lie spoken in his presence (though not the truth), and can no longer tell lies himself.

contracts.name: Wayward Guide
contracts.effect: The changeling alters signs to inflict the Lost Condition on one mortal per Glamour, or makes supernatural trackers win a Clash of Wills or lose his scent for the scene.
contracts.loophole: The changeling gets herself confused too, gaining the Confused Condition.
